hitchens_question: When the Pentagon's chief technology officer solemnly intones about AI companies' "responsibility" to prevent exploitation of their models while simultaneously funding Dutch factories to mass-produce combat robots for Ukraine, are we witnessing genuine ethical concern or the exquisite hypocrisy of an empire that wants its cake both eaten and remaining? The same military establishment that shoots down Iranian drones over international waters while wringing its hands about the "fragility" of its own rocket programs now lectures Silicon Valley on moral guardrails—as if the moral problem with artificial intelligence were not its subordination to the ancient human appetite for dominion, but rather insufficient supervision by the very institution whose business model depends on the efficient application of violence. Tell me: which is more corrupting to the human spirit—the honest brutality of a submunition, or the sanctimonious efficiency of an algorithm that kills with a clear conscience?
